verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted cysteine-rich glycoprotein produced by epididymal epithelium with no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or covalent attachment to a TM partner. It is secreted into the epididymal lumen and associates non-covalently with the sperm surface (loosely-bound Protein D form washes off during capacitation; tightly-bound Protein E persists on sperm post-capacitation). This surface association occurs in trans on a non-producing target cell (sperm) and is not mediated by CRISP1's own membrane-anchoring mechanism. No evidence of cell-surface-directed therapeutics or somatic cell surface expression was found. Verdict: secreted_only.
